title: ML lifecycle, roles, team topology + DSA Adv Graphs
focus: End-to-end map of failure modes; researcher vs MLE vs MLOps.

items.label: Min Cost TO Connect All Points
Lifecycle: data → train → eval → deploy → monitor → retrain
interviewQuestions: Walk through the end-to-end ML lifecycle and the failure modes at each stage.
Where do most ML projects actually fail in the lifecycle, and what catches it earlier?

items.label: ML roles: research, applied, MLE, MLOps, platform
interviewQuestions: Compare the responsibilities of an ML researcher vs an MLE vs an MLOps engineer.
When does a company actually need a dedicated ML platform team — and what's the smallest valid platform?

items.label: Team topology & ownership boundaries
interviewQuestions: How would you structure an ML team at a 50-person startup vs a 5,000-person company?
Where do ownership disputes typically erupt between data, ML, and platform teams — and how do you preempt them?

tracks.label: Company Loop · What different employers test

items.label: Meta-style loop: fast coding, product ML design, production follow-ups, behavioral
interviewQuestions: What rounds should you expect in a Meta-style MLE loop, and how do coding and ML design differ?
How would you answer a vague feed-ranking prompt while keeping product metrics, training data, and serving constraints visible?
What common failure shows up when candidates know ML theory but cannot connect it to a product surface?

items.label: Google-style loop: ML fundamentals, scalable systems, experimentation, responsible AI
interviewQuestions: How do Google-style interviews test metrics interpretation, data pipelines, and responsible AI together?
What production signals would you discuss for training-serving skew, attribution drift, and fairness readiness?
How would you prepare a concise story about collaborating with data, infra, and product roles?

items.label: AI-lab loop: technical depth, pair coding, evals, model behavior, safety, fast ramp
interviewQuestions: What does a skills-based assessment test beyond memorized ML facts?
How would you prepare to discuss a system you built at implementation, evaluation, and failure-mode depth?
What does strong communication look like when the interviewer stretches you beyond your comfort zone?

items.label: Amazon/AWS-style loop: operational ML, security, cloud workflows, ownership stories
interviewQuestions: How would you frame an ML workflow around data preparation, model development, deployment, and monitoring?
Which security and access-control decisions matter in a cloud-hosted ML platform?
What leadership story shows you owned a production ML failure end to end?

items.label: Startup loop: practical take-home, messy notebooks, MVP trade-offs, week-one execution
interviewQuestions: How do startup ML interviews compress coding, system design, debugging, and product judgment into one loop?
What would you build in week one if the company has no eval harness and a customer demo in five days?
How do you decide between a quick hosted API integration and building a reusable internal platform?
